statement: The European Union has 27 member states.
explanation: Croatia joined as the 27th member; the UK left in 2020.

statement: The United Nations was founded in 1945.
explanation: The UN Charter took effect on 24 October 1945.

statement: Antarctica has no permanent human population.
explanation: Only researchers live there, on rotating tours.

statement: Voting is compulsory in Australia.
explanation: Citizens face a small fine for not voting.

statement: The Berlin Wall fell in 1989.
explanation: It was opened on 9 November 1989, ending 28 years of separation.

statement: Greece is considered the birthplace of democracy.
explanation: Athens developed the first known democratic system around 500 BCE.

statement: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights has 30 articles.
explanation: It was adopted by the UN General Assembly in 1948.

statement: Switzerland regularly holds national citizen referendums.
explanation: Federal votes happen up to four times a year.

statement: The UN Security Council has five permanent members.
explanation: China, France, Russia, the UK and the US.

statement: Smallpox is the only human disease ever fully eradicated.
explanation: WHO declared it eradicated worldwide in 1980.

statement: South Africa held its first multi-racial election in 1994.
explanation: Nelson Mandela was elected president that year.

statement: The Internet has its roots in a US Department of Defense project.
explanation: ARPANET, predecessor of the modern Internet, launched in 1969.

statement: Saudi Arabia first allowed women to vote in 2015.
explanation: Women voted in municipal elections that December.

statement: All EU citizens can vote in European Parliament elections.
explanation: Direct elections have been held every five years since 1979.

statement: The Nobel Peace Prize is awarded annually in Oslo, Norway.
explanation: All other Nobel prizes are awarded in Stockholm, Sweden.

statement: The United Nations has 250 member states.
explanation: The UN actually has 193 member states.

statement: Humans only use 10 percent of their brain.
explanation: Brain imaging shows nearly all regions are active over a day.

statement: The Great Wall of China is visible from the Moon.
explanation: It cannot be seen with the naked eye even from low Earth orbit.

statement: Vaccines cause autism.
explanation: Decades of research find no link; the original 1998 paper was retracted.

statement: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
explanation: Tall structures like the Empire State Building are hit dozens of times per year.

statement: Antibiotics work against the common cold.
explanation: Colds are viral; antibiotics only treat bacterial infections.

statement: All European countries use the Euro.
explanation: Sweden, Denmark, Switzerland, Norway and others do not use the Euro.

statement: 5G networks spread viruses.
explanation: Radio waves cannot carry biological viruses.

statement: The Sahara has always been a desert.
explanation: Until about 5,000 years ago much of it was green grassland and lakes.

statement: Climate change is not happening.
explanation: Global temperatures and CO2 levels have been rising for over a century.

statement: The voting age is 21 in every democracy.
explanation: Most democracies set it at 18; some allow voting at 16.

statement: The European Union has a single, directly elected president.
explanation: The EU has multiple presidents and none are elected directly by all citizens.

statement: Drinking 8 glasses of water a day is required by health law.
explanation: There is no such law; needs vary by person and climate.

statement: The United Nations was founded in 1900.
explanation: The UN was founded in 1945, after World War II.

statement: Australia is larger than Russia.
explanation: Russia is roughly twice the area of Australia.
